content.question: Do non-structural Hungarian equity interventions (targeted staffing, training, funding formulas) alone demonstrably close rural attainment or retention gaps without any consolidation, and if so, by what magnitude and for which subpopulations?
content.reduction_path: Commission a causal evaluation comparing Hungarian rural districts with varying intensities of non-structural support (multigrade training, salary incentives, professional networks) and matched non-intervention controls, controlling for prior enrollment trend and SES composition; measure attainment and retention effects over 3-5 years.
